title: Synthetic supplier invoice approval
description: A non-operational example for discussing the schema. It labels a fictional training-fixture invoice and does not authorize any payment or represent a real accounts-payable policy.

decision.intent: Determine whether a fictional supplier invoice may be automatically approved, sent to manual review, escalated, or rejected in a training-fixture context.
decision.question: Should this supplier invoice be approved automatically?

evidenceRequirements.description: An invented matching purchase order for the fictional invoice.
An invented goods receipt confirming the fictional order was received in full.
The required tax information for the fictional supplier and invoice.

sources.title: Fictional supplier invoice approval checklist

citation.excerpt: An invoice with a matching purchase order, a complete goods receipt, required tax information, and an amount within tolerance may follow the automatic path. Restricted suppliers are rejected.

sources.rights: Invented content for specification testing; not an operational policy.

outcomes.label: Approve
outcomes.description: The invented evidence and declared rules support the automatic path in the fictional scenario.

outcomes.label: Manual review
outcomes.description: An accountable accounts-payable reviewer must decide.

outcomes.label: Escalate
outcomes.description: The fictional variance exceeds the declared threshold and needs a person to look at it.

outcomes.label: Reject
outcomes.description: The fictional supplier is marked restricted.

rules.description: Reject the fictional invoice when its supplier is marked restricted.
rules.rationale: The example checklist rejects invoices from restricted suppliers.

rules.description: Escalate the fictional invoice when its amount variance exceeds the declared tolerance.
rules.rationale: The example checklist sends variances above the tolerance to a person.

rules.description: Approve a supported fictional invoice within tolerance when all required evidence is present and the supplier is not restricted.
rules.rationale: Ordinary supported invoices within tolerance may follow the automatic path.

exceptions.description: A fictional invoice flagged as a suspected duplicate requires human review.

escalation.message: Review only synthetic training inputs: the invoice, the matching purchase order, the goods receipt, tax information, the cited checklist, missing evidence, and conflicting rules; do not release any payment.
